ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART. AS TO REQUESTS: #10, 11, 12, 13, AND 16 DEFENDANT HAS AGREED TO PRODUCE THE DOCUMENTS. THE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS SHALL BE PRODUCED BY 11/24/04. AS TO THE REMAINING REQUESTS, THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH THE LIMITATION THAT RECORDS FROM 1995 TO THE PRESENT SHALL BE PRODUCED. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE VERIFICATION THAT ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS PRODUCED. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

Minor'S Compromise In Scd.
Off Calendar Sep-27-2005 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Minor's Compromise hearing this day.
Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Leticia Perez.
Bruce Nelson, Esq. for Petitioner: Rosalyn Mathis, present as Guardian at litem for: Teresa Carter and Diane Lipkins, minor Claimants, was sworn and examined.
On the basis of evidence received, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.
No Fees or Costs.
A net balance of $8,000.00 for each Minor to be deposited into a blocked account with US. Bank, 4947 3rd St., San Francisco, CA., no withdrawals shall be made without a written court order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.
Orders Approving Compromise of Claim and Orders to Deposit Money Into Blocked Account signed in open court.

Mtn For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-28-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bruce A. Nelson for plaintiff and moving party: Dorothy Lipkins
Laura Sagmeister-Flynn for defendant and opposing party: Bayview Property Managers
Court Reporter: Joanna Broadwell CSR#10959

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for terminating, evidentiary, and monetary sanctions is denied in part and granted in part.

The motion for monetary sanctions is granted. The Court finds that defendant did not produce the specified documents pursuant to the June 1, 2005 order by May 26, 2005. The Court makes no findings as to the sufficiency of the production. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $831.80 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.

The motion for terminating or evidentiary sanctions is denied. The previous rulings of the Court permitted discovery of the financial condition of defendant Bayview Property Managers. The Court is unable to make any rulings as to the propriety of particular questions asked during deposition absent a motion to compel pursuant to CCP Section 2025 (m). ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar May-12-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bruce Nelson, Esq., 510/452-0500, appearing for plaintiff/moving party
Laura Sagmeister-Flynn, Esq. of DRATH, CLIFFORD, MURPHY & HAGEN 510/287-4000, appearing for defendant/opposing parties Bayview Property Managers and James F. Blanding

RULING:
Argued and TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to special interrogatories set #1 (Blanding) --
1, 2: denied. The objections are overruled and stricken. The answer is otherwise sufficient
3: granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to supplement the response stating facts that support the specified contention.
4: granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to provide the last known address and telephone number of any former employees.
5: denied. The objections are overruled and stricken.
6 - 8: granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to further respond.
9: granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to identify any and all policies that relate to the years 1995 to 2000.
36 - 45: granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. Defendant is ordered to further respond

The Court finds that defendant asserted a number of unmeritorious objections to the special interrogatories set #1 propounded to defendants Bayview and Blanding. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1036.30 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.

Further responses shall be provided by May 26, 2005. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Conduct Pre Trial Discovery
Off Calendar Apr-26-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bruce Nelson for plaintiff and moving party
Laura Flynn for defendant and opposing party
Easteller Bruihl, Reporter, CSR #3077
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modifications: Plaintiff's motion for leave to conduct pre-trial discovery of defendants' financial condition is granted. Counsel for plaintiff may not disclose or permit to be disclosed any information as to the defendants' financial condition for purposes unrelated to this litigation. Said information may be disclosed to persons employed by counsel to assist in the preparation of the case, if that disclosure is required for that preparation. Persons provided this information by counsel shall be informed that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order.
MODIFICATION: The motion DENIED without prejudice as to defendant Blanding.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
